history.post_text:
Had to tell a senior engineer this week that his code reviews were killing team morale.

Not the technical feedback — that was solid. It was the tone. Dismissive. Impatient. Making juniors feel stupid for asking questions.

He had no idea.

Took him for chai, showed him three examples. His face changed. "I thought I was being efficient," he said.

Here's the thing: being right isn't enough. How you deliver matters as much as what you deliver.

We agreed on a reset. More context in comments. Assume good intent. Treat every review like you're teaching, not gatekeeping.

Two days later, a junior pinged me: "His review actually helped me understand the why."

That's the job. Not just shipping features — shaping how your team treats each other. 🛠️

#EngineeringLeadership #TeamCulture

pendingDraft.postText:
Had a tough conversation with one of my iOS leads yesterday. Performance dropped 20% over two sprints. His manager wanted to put him on a PIP immediately.

I pushed back. Sat with him for an hour instead. Turns out he's been debugging a gnarly memory leak solo for weeks—no one knew. He didn't ask for help because he thought he'd figure it out.

That's on me as a leader, not him.

Moved him to pair with our Android expert. Three days later, issue closed. Back to shipping.

The hard feedback wasn't about performance. It was about communication. He needed to know that asking for help is how we work here, not a weakness 🛠️

Culture isn't built on what you say in all-hands. It's built on how you respond when someone struggles.

#EngineeringLeadership #TeamBuilding #StartupLife
